mission.title: Install and Configure Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE)
mission.description: Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Linode's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a hassle-free Kubernetes experience without the overhead of managing the underlying hardware. Key trade-offs include limited customization compared to self-managed clusters and potential costs associated with resource usage.

steps.title: Install the Linode CLI
steps.description:
The Linode CLI is required to interact with the Linode API for creating and managing your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -sL https://github.com/linode/linode-cli/releases/download/v2.0.0/linode-cli-2.0.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz | tar xz -C /usr/local/bin

chmod +x /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> This installs the Linode CLI binary.
> Make the CLI executable.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Linode with the specified version, node count, and region.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-create --label my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3 --region us-east

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your desired cluster name.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This command configures your local kubeconfig file to access the newly created Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster --format yaml > ~/.kube/config

```

> Ensure you have the correct permissions to write to ~/.kube/config.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster to ensure they are running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster to enhance its functionality and security.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/linode/linode-cloud-controller-manager/v0.9.5/deploy/ccm-linode-template.yaml

```

> This applies the necessary configurations for the Linode Cloud Controller Manager.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-delete my-k8s-cluster

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.

uninstall.title: Remove the Linode CLI
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Linode CLI from your system.

```bash

rm /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> Ensure you have the necessary permissions to remove the binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your data and ensure your applications are compatible with the new version.

```bash

linode-cli k8s cluster-update my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Replace 'my-k8s-cluster' with your actual cluster name.
> Verify that all nodes are in 'Ready' status after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This error indicates that your kubeconfig is not correctly set up or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Ensure your kubeconfig file is correctly configured and points to the right cluster context.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image: ...'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the specified container image cannot be found or accessed.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it exists in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources allocated to schedule new pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or size in your cluster configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Node is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the node is not in a healthy state, possibly due to resource constraints or network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the node status and logs for any errors, and ensure it has sufficient resources.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features including improved scheduling and resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated certain legacy APIs that are no longer recommended for use.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.29, ensure your applications are compatible with the new API changes.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get nodes' showing all nodes in 'Ready' status, indicating the cluster is operational.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the Linode CLI installed and configured with your API token.
